title: anthriscus sylvestris subsp. alpina
intro: Anthriscus sylvestris subsp. alpina, also known as wild chervil or alpine chervil, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is known to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Anthriscus sylvestris subsp. alpina can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in cooking as a culinary herb, adding a mild onion-like flavor to various dishes.
